content.method: Time-use audit of specialist staff pre/post a piloted organizational redesign (e.g., decentralizing assessment scheduling or reallocating administrative tasks) in a sample of districts, using before-after comparison with matched control districts retaining current centralized structure.
content.question: What is the current specialist capacity gap and would restructuring the 2013 KLIK-centralized service model reverse the documented shift of specialist time from therapy toward assessment, without requiring additional total staffing?
content.why_it_matters: This tests whether organizational redesign alone (a low-cost lever) can recover therapeutic capacity, which would materially change the cost-effectiveness case for T5 versus more expensive workforce-expansion-only approaches.
